question: What is the definition of the abbreviation RCS?

nuscale_ch01_0701.title: DSRS 15.1.6, Rev 0: Loss of Containment VacuumII.4 Analytical ParametersMaximize Heat Transfer from RCS to Containment and Reactor Pool
nuscale_ch01_0701.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various accident scenarios, including loss of containment vacuum and related analytical parameters. It confirms that NuScale’s design meets the requirements for maximizing heat transfer from the reactor coolant system (RCS) to the containment and reactor pool, as well as other parameters, with no exceptions noted. For some events, such as steam pressure regulator failure, partial conformance is noted due to design differences.

nuscale_ch01_0786.title: DSRS 15.6.6, Rev 0: Inadvertent Operation of ECCSII.1 RCS pressure below 110 percent design value.
nuscale_ch01_0786.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding the inadvertent operation of ECCS when RCS pressure is below 110 percent of the design value. It concludes that NuScale fully conforms to the relevant DSRS 15.6.6 requirements for both RCS pressure and maintaining minimum DNBR, while noting only partial conformance to some SRP 15.6.5 radiological consequence criteria due to superseding guidance in SRP 15.0.3. Certain criteria are also identified as not applicable because they pertain only to BWRs.

nuscale_ch01_0748.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.2 Fuel Cladding Integrity
nuscale_ch01_0748.summary: The section explains that the NuScale reactor design conforms to the regulatory criteria for fuel cladding integrity in the event of an inadvertent decrease in boron concentration, as outlined in SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2. It notes that "CHF" (Critical Heat Flux) is the appropriate term for describing the relevant phenomenon in NuScale's design, and confirms that the analysis methods and assumptions meet the required standards.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis diagrams. It defines the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols to ensure clear interpretation of system status and operations in the diagrams.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for symbols used in NuScale's Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the graphical representations for various instruments, sensors, and auxiliary devices. It includes symbols for sample probes, pressure and temperature sensors, flow measurement devices, and level indicators, ensuring consistent interpretation of P&ID drawings.

nuscale_ch01_0747.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.1 Reactor Coolant and Main Steam System Pressures
nuscale_ch01_0747.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2, regarding inadvertent decreases in boron concentration in the reactor coolant system for PWRs. It states that NuScale’s design meets the relevant regulatory criteria for reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, fuel cladding integrity, incident frequency, and analysis methods, but notes that some guidance is not applicable due to the absence of reactor coolant pumps and the lack of required operator action for boron dilution events.
